hero_title_accent: For VASPs and Digital Asset Teams
hero_subtitle: SFTY structures and connects fragmented provider outputs into auditable onboarding workflows that apply rules, capture decisions, and preserve compliance evidence for streamlined review.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_description: Compliance teams rely on multiple disconnected KYC, KYB and screening providers, resulting in slow and duplicated onboarding reviews. This fragmentation makes it difficult to enforce consistent policies, maintain audit-ready evidence and trace decision history effectively.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY links KYC, KYB and screening provider outputs into a unified workflow that automates decision routing, preserves audit evidence and enforces consistent onboarding policies across teams.
workflow_description: SFTY consolidates fragmented provider checks and applies internal rules to route reviews, create cases, record decisions and preserve audit-ready onboarding evidence.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. One onboarding control
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects provider outputs into structured onboarding workflows, decisions, cases and audit-ready evidence without replacing the tools teams already use.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs with connected onboarding workflows that preserve audit evidence, enforce decision rules and prepare clear compliance records for bank due diligence.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support connected onboarding decision workflows
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how onboarding checks become auditable case evidence
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how internal rules control onboarding review workflows
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe provider orchestration for multi-vendor onboarding management
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for VASP compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY acts as a compliance control layer for VASPs, connecting KYC, KYB and other outputs into structured workflows and audit-ready evidence without replacing providers.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY connect onboarding workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY connects onboarding by aggregating provider outputs, applying internal rules, routing reviews, managing cases and logging decisions in one controllable workflow.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve onboarding audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves onboarding evidence by recording case history, decision logs and provider results, supporting audit readiness and defensible review of compliance actions.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate existing KYC and KYB providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY integrates with your existing KYC and KYB providers, connecting their outputs into one operational system without requiring replacement.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace our existing compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No, SFTY does not replace providers; it connects their data into structured workflows, supporting audit trails and reporting workflows while reducing manual workload.
final_title: Structure onboarding decisions with SFTY controls
final_description: Review how SFTY connects fragmented provider outputs into rule-based workflows, decision logs and audit-ready onboarding evidence.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
